name: Harissa Lentil & Chickpea Shepherd's Pie
description: A smoky, harissa-led shepherd's pie with split red lentils, chickpeas, air-fried courgette and mushroom, under a 50/50 potato and sweet potato mash. Improves on day two.

ingredients.name: rose harissa
ingredients.notes: Belazu is the benchmark — start with 2 tbsp if yours is fiercer

ingredients.name: red or yellow split lentils, rinsed
sherry vinegar or red wine vinegar

steps.title: Boil the potatoes
steps.content: Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Put {0001} and {0002} into a large pan, cover with cold salted water, bring to the boil, then simmer until tender — around 18–20 minutes.

steps.title: Toss the veg for the air fryer
steps.content: In a bowl, toss {0006} and {0007} with {0008}, {0009}, {0010} and {0011}. Make sure everything is well coated.

steps.title: Air fry the veg
steps.content: Spread the courgette and mushrooms into the Ninja air fryer basket — don't overcrowd; do two batches if needed. Cook at 200°C for 14 minutes, shaking halfway. You want proper colour: golden edges on the courgette, mushrooms shrunk and glossy. Set aside.

steps.title: Sweat the onion
steps.content: While the veg are in the air fryer, heat {0012} in a wide, deep pan over medium-low heat. Add {0013} with a pinch of salt and cook gently until soft and translucent — around 8 minutes. Don't rush this; it's the foundation.

steps.title: Bloom the spices
steps.content: Add {0014}, {0015}, {0016}, {0017} and {0018}. Stir constantly for about 90 seconds until everything smells fragrant and the harissa has darkened the oil.

steps.title: Cook down the tomatoes
steps.content: Add {0019} and turn the heat up. Let them bubble and reduce hard — you're compensating for not using tomato purée here, so push it for about 5 minutes until the tomatoes thicken and turn a deeper, brick-red colour.

steps.title: Simmer the lentils
steps.content: Stir in {0020}, {0021} and {0022}. Bring to a gentle simmer, cover loosely, and cook for around 18 minutes until the lentils have broken down and the base is thick. Stir occasionally to stop it catching on the bottom.

steps.title: Make the mash
steps.content: Drain the potatoes and sweet potatoes, return them to the pan, and mash with {0003} and {0004}. Season generously with {0005}. You want it firm enough to hold its shape on top of the filling, not sloppy.

steps.title: Finish the base
steps.content: Stir {0023} into the lentil base. Add {0024} and check seasoning — it should taste savoury, smoky, with a quiet sharpness from the vinegar. Adjust salt and harissa to taste.

steps.title: Fold in the air-fried veg
steps.content: Tip the air-fried {0006} and {0007} into the base along with {0025}. Fold gently — you want them held intact, not crushed. The filling should be thick and scoopable, not soupy. If it looks too wet, simmer uncovered for a few more minutes.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.content: Transfer the filling into a baking dish (roughly 25 x 20cm or similar). Spoon the mash on top in dollops, then spread to cover. Rough the surface up with a fork — those peaks crisp up beautifully in the oven.

steps.title: Bake
steps.content: Bake at 200°C fan for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden and crisp and the filling is bubbling at the edges.

steps.title: Rest before serving
steps.content: Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving — it'll firm up and the flavours settle. Serve with your broccoli or cabbage on the side.

notes:
**Side veg.** Broccoli works well steamed or, better, charred in the air fryer (200°C, 8–10 min with olive oil, salt, and a pinch of chilli) — slot it in while the pie rests. Cabbage is great shredded and fried hard in a little olive oil with caraway or cumin seeds until the edges catch and crisp.

**Day two.** This improves overnight. Reheat individual portions in the oven at 180°C for 20 minutes covered, then uncovered for the last 5 to re-crisp the top. Alternatively, assemble two smaller dishes and only bake one — the unbaked one keeps better in the fridge and reheats cleaner.

**Consistency check.** If the filling looks too wet before the mash goes on, simmer it uncovered for a few more minutes — too much liquid will bubble up through the mash. If it looks too thick, splash in a little more stock.

**Heat level.** Rose harissa varies a lot by brand. 3 tbsp gives a warm, fragrant heat with Belazu. If yours is fiercer, start with 2 tbsp and taste before adding more.
